activities_projects.title: Surface Area of Combined Solids
activities_projects.objective: Calculate surface area of two or more solids joined together
steps: Take a cylinder mounted on a cube
Calculate individual surface areas
Subtract the area of the overlapping part
Sum the remaining surface areas for total surface area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies surface area concept to combined solids

activities_projects.title: Volume of Composite Solids
activities_projects.objective: Find volume of solids formed by combining two or more solids
steps: Take a cuboid with a hemisphere on top
Calculate cuboid volume
Calculate hemisphere volume
Add both volumes to get total

activities_projects.title: Real-life Application: Water Tank
activities_projects.objective: Calculate surface area and volume of a cylindrical water tank
steps: Measure radius and height of tank
Calculate curved surface area and total surface area
Calculate volume to find water capacity

activities_projects.title: Conversion of Units
activities_projects.objective: Solve surface area and volume problems with mixed units
steps: Take cylinder with radius in cm, height in m
Convert both to same unit
Calculate surface area and volume

activities_projects.title: Volume of Hollow Solids
activities_projects.objective: Calculate volume of hollow cylinders and cones
steps: Take hollow cylinder with outer radius 7 cm, inner radius 5 cm, height 10 cm
Use formula V = πh(R² - r²) to calculate volume
Record calculation

activities_projects.title: Finding Cost of Material
activities_projects.objective: Calculate cost of paint required for covering a solid
steps: Take a cube with side 5 m
Calculate total surface area
If paint costs 50 per m², calculate total cost
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects surface area to real-life cost estimation

activities_projects.title: Packing Problems
activities_projects.objective: Calculate number of smaller solids that can fit in a larger solid
steps: Take large cube of side 10 m
Take small cubes of side 2 m
Calculate volume of both
Determine how many small cubes can fit

activities_projects.title: Surface Area of Frustum of Cone
activities_projects.objective: Calculate lateral and total surface area of a frustum
steps: Take frustum with radii 5 cm and 3 cm, height 7 cm
Calculate slant height l = √(h² + (R-r)²)
Calculate lateral surface area = πl(R+r)
Add areas of two circular ends for total surface area

activities_projects.title: Volume of Cone and Sphere Combination
activities_projects.objective: Find total volume when a cone is placed on a hemisphere
steps: Take cone radius 7 cm, height 10 cm and hemisphere radius 7 cm
Calculate cone volume = 1/3 πr²h
Calculate hemisphere volume = 2/3 πr³
Sum both volumes for total

activities_projects.title: Practical Problem: Water in Tank with Cover
activities_projects.objective: Calculate water volume and surface area for covering tank
steps: Take cylindrical water tank with radius 3 m, height 5 m
Calculate curved surface area and total surface area
Calculate volume of water it can hold
Determine paint needed for tank cover
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies SA and volume formulas in real-life context
